title: Scrofae - Better Piglins
description: Improved Piglin and Piglin Brute textures based on biome.
body:
![variants](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9526d9a3de8d15811b570d98b37b00e2027a0526.png)
Piglin examples from each biome ![biomes](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/vPcIDv1e/images/f9a52bb5dd425c25e880e7d3d16b59da582dbb43.png)
Piglin Brute examples from each biome ![biomes](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/vPcIDv1e/images/94b57850df695d195471de965757c2df84df908b.png )
_Updates all piglin related textures to a more naturalistic texture. Piglins will now have biome dependent texture based on the evolutionary pressure of the specific biome. Roughly ten variants per biome ensure a more realistic horde. Piglins now also have their own more savage swords, axes, and spears made out of bone and gold._ ___ ### The Nether Waste The Nether Waste piglins are the standard piglins without optifine. They are opportunistic scavangers of the barren wastes, and are more often seen with clothing and gold than the other tribes. ### The Crimson Forest The Crimson Forest's lush red mycelium carpet is home to numerous piglin tribes in constant conflict. The adults tattoo themselves to scare off enemies, and the babies are covered in pigments from the forest floor to camouflage them from intruders. ### The Warped Forest The Warped Forest contains the lepers of piglin society. Slowly corrupted by the warped spores in the air; their skin has faded to an ashen grey, and fatigue underlines their crazed eyes. ### The Soulsand Valley The Soulsand Valley's harse conditions has forced the recluses and exiles who live there to adapt in order to survive. Naturally camouflaged; their skin covered by the cursed dust swept by the howling wind through the caverous gulch. They mimic the skeletons who hunt them with bodypaint in their image to truly glide into the scenery. ___ Since neither warped forests nor soulsand valleys allow piglins to spawn, these will primarily be encountered in bastions in said biomes. ___ You might also like [Pyr](https://modrinth.com/datapack/pyr) or just like the portal textures: [](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/legendary-portals)[](https://modrinth.com/project/improved-portals)
